turns.text: editmore_vertPlease combine this original video and it's sound with the new sound file. Mix the original video sound down to about 20% of the original volume and keep the new sound file volume at 100%.

more_vertModel Thoughts Evaluating Video PotentialI'm currently focused on the video's potential. My initial analysis reveals a recurring visual narrative: a monolithic block that cracks and shatters, replaced by a new rising block that repeats the pattern. The sound effects of rumbling and stone are integral to the experience.Adding the Musical ScoreI've introduced a new audio track to the mix, a dark hip-hop/trap beat that perfectly mirrors the video's visual theme. The lyrics about "silent drops" and "tectonic shifts" align with the shattering stone imagery. I'm now setting up the technical requirements. I plan to overlay the music, reducing the original sound effects of stone to 20% volume while keeping the new track at 100%. The video and audio durations are nearly identical, so I will synchronize them. I will load the video file, extract the original audio, load the new audio, adjust the volumes, and mix the streams.Completing the IntegrationMy plan is now to merge the refined audio track with the video stream, finalizing the project. I've considered all details. The video and audio durations are perfectly synchronized at around 2 minutes and 22 seconds, a perfect match. I'm focusing on the implementation. Using ffmpeg, the process will involve reducing the original stone sounds, mixing with the music, and generating the final output. The goal is a clear music experience enriched by subtle stone sound effects. Expand to view model thoughts  chevron_right thumb_upthumb_down
